claim: Gemini 2.0 Flash can generate native audio output in multiple languages, not just text-to-speech, allowing control over how text is spoken (tone, emphasis, style)
actionable: Use Gemini 2.0 Flash's native audio feature to generate spoken content in various languages with natural prosody and controlled delivery style for multilingual applications

claim: Gemini 2.0 Flash can generate multiple images inline within text responses, not using external models, enabling interactive workflows like showing recipe steps with images
actionable: Prompt Gemini 2.0 Flash to generate text responses with embedded images for mixed-media outputs; use for interactive tutorials, product mockups, and multi-step visual guides

claim: Gemini 2.0 Flash supports conversational image editing where you can upload an image, describe changes, and the model generates modified versions conditioned on both image and text prompt
actionable: Use Gemini 2.0 Flash for iterative image editing tasks: upload image → describe edits → receive modified image → refine further, enabling rapid visual design iteration

claim: Gemini 2.0 Flash's multimodal live API enables real-time bidirectional streaming for voice conversations and video interactions, supporting interruption and on-the-fly redirection
actionable: Implement real-time conversational agents using the multimodal live API for voice chatbots, live translation, gaming interactions, and streaming video analysis with natural interruption handling

claim: Gemini 2.0 Flash's unified SDK consolidates AI Studio and Vertex AI SDKs, allowing code to be developed in one environment and switched to the other by changing endpoints
actionable: Use the unified SDK to prototype in AI Studio, then deploy to Vertex AI by only changing the endpoint, enabling consistent development across prototyping and production
